source: CT6-6 §8 (SR-1…3) — the Session Review agent on the v3.23.0 substrate; session-level review + outbound summary push + the issues agents couldn't solve on the first attempt, reusing the triage record + sink

scenarios: a session is reviewed at the session level and is schedulable
acceptance_criteria: session-level review of a full session via the injected LLM (string-aware parse)
of similar design to the Librarian; runs on the shared bg_runtime
build_review_task returns a ServiceTask named session-review
install_descriptor returns a per-OS boot/restart descriptor

scenarios: off transmits nothing; an opt-in pushes the summary
acceptance_criteria: review_and_push performs a simple outbound summary push via an injected pusher
off (default) transmits NOTHING off-machine: no push, no filing (EVAL-17)
summary/full opts in to transmit; push is best-effort (raising pusher swallowed)

scenarios: a stringified-false issue is kept, a genuinely-solved one is dropped
acceptance_criteria: keep ONLY issues NOT solved on the first attempt
robust boolean coercion: stringified 'false' kept; missing kept; real true / 'true' / 1 dropped
kept issues normalized as REUSED triage issue records + filed through the triage sink
services/session_review stdlib-only core, design-not-deployed; LLM/push-target/persistence are adapters
full suite green cp1252 AND PYTHONUTF8=1

testing_authorizations.playwright: N/A — no UI/frontend surface (a stdlib-only background service + tests).
testing_authorizations.dev_api_integration: N/A — the real LLM + the live outbound-push target (the triage server) are external infra behind adapter boundaries (not in-repo); the deterministic core (review parse, SR-3 filter, push gating) is exercised by the pytest suite with FakeLLMClient + InMemorySink + an injected pusher.

confirmed_stubs.why: the live push target is the triage server; a real pusher signs the payload via services/common/handshake.py and POSTs it (the separated tier's own dep, REPO-4). The core injects the pusher (None -> not transmitted, honest) — an adapter boundary, not a gap
the real Anthropic call needs the SDK + network; the core injects an LLMClient (FakeLLMClient for tests) — the documented adapter boundary, not a gap
